sonnet_review.biasedText: In their swift march toward installing a kind of Oval Office monarchy over the past year
sonnet_review.reason: This matches my detection of the same passage as opinion presented as fact with sensational characterization. Spin is also an appropriate label.

sonnet_review.biasedText: the GOP wing attempted to insulate
sonnet_review.reason: While I didn't flag this specific phrase, 'GOP wing' is indeed a partisan framing of the Court's conservative majority. It's a minor but defensible detection.

sonnet_review.biasedText: The Republican-appointees rode to his rescue with a shocking new doctrine of presidential immunity
sonnet_review.reason: I flagged this same passage as Spin. The detection is valid—characterizing it as opinion presented as fact is also appropriate since 'rode to his rescue' assumes partisan motivation as fact.

sonnet_review.biasedText: The GOP-appointed justices foolishly thought they could secure the Fed's independence
sonnet_review.reason: I flagged 'foolishly' as a subjective qualifying adjective and separately flagged the mind-reading element. This detection captures both aspects.

sonnet_review.biasedText: It is the hubris of a group of people who think they can control a would-be authoritarian
sonnet_review.reason: Matches my detection exactly—attributing 'hubris' and specific strategic reasoning to justices without evidence.
I flagged the same passage, with 'infamous' as a subjective value judgment embedded in the author's own voice.
Matches my detection of this passage as mudslinging/ad hominem. Word choice is also appropriate.
I flagged the fuller sentence as an unsubstantiated claim. Characterizing it as spin is also valid since 'brainchild' is interpretive.

sonnet_review.biasedText: What could be more tempting to a wannabe king?
sonnet_review.reason: Matches my detection. 'Wannabe king' is a loaded characterization in the author's voice.

sonnet_review.biasedText: The idea that the justices could give Trump such a weapon but control where he aimed it was always absurd
sonnet_review.reason: Matches my detection exactly—'always absurd' is a subjective judgment presented as fact.

sonnet_review.biasedText: Trump's insatiable desire to control the levers of the economy
sonnet_review.reason: I didn't flag this specifically, but attributing an 'insatiable desire' does claim knowledge of Trump's psychological state. Defensible detection.

sonnet_review.biasedText: But this court's GOP-appointed majority has rushed breathlessly forward, not willing to contemplate either the ramifications of their own actions or the limits of their power
sonnet_review.reason: Matches my detection of this passage as spin with mind-reading elements.

gpt_review.biasedText: In their swift march toward installing a kind of Oval Office monarchy over the past year, the Supreme Court's Republican-appointed majority has signaled that it would give the president unfettered power over federal agencies, even ones Congress tried to carve out as independent from partisan politics.
gpt_review.reason: Matches my detection of this passage as opinion presented as fact with sensational framing.

gpt_review.biasedText: The GOP-appointed justices foolishly thought they could secure the Fed's independence.
The idea that the justices could empower Trump but also contain that power is a folly we see repeated throughout history.
gpt_review.reason: I didn't flag this exact sentence separately, but it does present a subjective historical judgment as established fact. Defensible.

gpt_review.biasedText: It is the hubris of a group of people who think they can control a would-be authoritarian; that he will be useful to their purposes without ever turning on them.
If you give someone like Trump every power but one, he will find a way to take the last one too.
gpt_review.reason: I didn't flag this separately, but it is a sweeping generalization about Trump's character presented as inevitable truth. Defensible.

gpt_review.biasedText: the Republican-appointees rode to his rescue with a shocking new doctrine of presidential immunity.
gpt_review.reason: Matches my detection of this passage.

gpt_review.biasedText:
Roberts' infamous decision in Trump v. United States did not just protect Trump from prosecution, it gave him the power to use criminal investigation and prosecution to harass, intimidate, impoverish, and coerce anyone
us2014even with sham prosecutions ginned up for political purposes.
gpt_review.reason: Matches my detection. 'Infamous' is the subjective qualifier, and the rest of the passage contains opinion as fact.

gpt_review.biasedText: Now, he is using that weapon to take control of the Fed.
gpt_review.reason: I didn't flag this exact sentence, but it does assert as fact a contested interpretation of events. Defensible.

gpt_review.biasedText: Prosecutions no longer need to be tethered to reality.
gpt_review.reason: I didn't flag this specifically, but it is a sweeping interpretive claim presented without attribution. Defensible.

gpt_review.biasedText: the president himself is unconstrained, while politically-motivated prosecutions hound his critics.
gpt_review.reason: I didn't flag this exact phrase, but 'unconstrained' and 'hound' are dramatic interpretive word choices. Defensible.

gpt_review.biasedText: while a criminal case against her proceeds—one that likewise appears to be bogus and inspired by a desire to take control of the Fed.
gpt_review.reason: I didn't flag this exact passage, but calling the case 'bogus' is a judgment by the author. Defensible.

gpt_review.biasedText: Frighteningly, the Fed does much more than set interest rates, and control of its bottomless money supply and other powers could transform it into a slush fund to reward loyalists and family members and to de-bank and financially destroy Trump's enemies.
gpt_review.reason: Matches my detection of this passage as sensationalism and emotionalism.

gpt_review.biasedText: What could be more tempting to a wannabe king?
gpt_review.reason: Matches my detection. 'Wannabe king' is a loaded characterization.

gpt_review.biasedText: The all-powerful presidency envisioned by the unitary executive theory, by contrast, mimics authoritarian takeovers in countries like Turkey and Hungary where democracy has recently eroded, giving an intellectual gloss to what looks, under Trump, to be a constitutional coup.
gpt_review.reason: Matches my detection of 'constitutional coup' as loaded word choice.

gpt_review.biasedText: But Roberts and his colleagues have plowed ahead, using Trump's power grabs to create their long-sought uber-powerful presidency.
gpt_review.reason: I didn't flag this exact sentence, but 'plowed ahead,' 'power grabs,' and 'uber-powerful presidency' are all dramatic and interpretive. Defensible.

gpt_review.biasedText: Roberts' opinions justified this expansion of presidential power on the grounds that the president was uniquely democratically accountable; but predictably, the more power he has given Trump, the less accountable he has become.
gpt_review.reason: I didn't flag this exact passage, but 'predictably' does embed the author's interpretive judgment. Defensible.

gpt_review.biasedText: It also shows how silly Trump's allies in robes have been in assuming that Trump would stay within the limits they set for him.
gpt_review.reason: Matches my detection of 'Trump's allies in robes' as loaded word choice.

gpt_review.biasedText: But this court's GOP-appointed majority has rushed breathlessly forward, not willing to contemplate either the ramifications of their own actions or the limits of their power.
gpt_review.reason: Matches my detection of this passage.

sonnet_false_negatives.reason: Sonnet missed this passage which uses 'Frighteningly' as an emotional appeal and presents speculative worst-case scenarios as likely outcomes.

Sonnet did not note the complete absence of opposing perspectives—no legal scholars defending the Court's decisions, no DOJ perspective, no defenders of unitary executive theory.

gpt_false_negatives.reason: GPT missed this direct personal insult in the author's voice, calling a government official a 'toady.'
